title: Phase 2: SDK + Hello World
description: C SDK toolchain (linker script, entry trampoline, paperdos.h, pdpack.py) and first Hello World .pdb app

title: EPIC-P2-A: C SDK Toolchain
description: paperdos.ld linker script, pd_entry.S trampoline, paperdos.h C header, pdpack.py ELF-to-.pdb packager

title: BR-P2-A1 pdpack.py header-layout unit tests
description: sdk/tools/test_pdpack.py: build_pdb() with zero-byte image produces 104-byte output with correct magic and CRC32; validate_pdb() on that output returns (True, msgs). pytest passes.

title: BR-P2-A2 pdpack.py relocation-extraction unit tests
description: extract_relocations(elf_data, sections): synthetic ELF32-LE with .rela.text containing two R_RISCV_32 (type=1) and one R_RISCV_64 (type=2). Assert: returns sorted list of exactly the two type-1 offsets.

title: BR-P2-A3 pdpack.py round-trip build+validate
description: 8-byte image with 2 reloc entries: build_pdb then validate_pdb -> (True,_). Mutate one byte -> validate_pdb -> (False,_) (CRC mismatch). Both assertions pass.

title: BR-P2-A4 paperdos.h struct-layout compile-check
description: sdk/tools/test_header_layout.c: _Static_assert sizeof(pd_syscalls_t)==16+52*4, offsetof display_clear==16, sizeof pd_dirent_t==264. gcc -std=c11 -fsyntax-only exits 0.

title: BR-P2-A5 pd_entry.S assembler smoke-check
description: riscv32-esp-elf-gcc -march=rv32imc -c pd_entry.S exits 0. Python test asserts pd_entry.S contains '0xAC(s0)' (sys_exit offset) and 'jalr' instruction.

title: EPIC-P2-B: Hello World App
description: Hello World C app built with SDK, packaged as .pdb, validated by pdpack.py, run on hardware/emulator

title: BR-P2-B1 Canonical hello_world C source
description: sdk/examples/hello_world/main.c: pd_main calls exactly display_clear(0xFF), display_draw_text(10,10,...,NULL), display_refresh(0), input_wait_button(0), sys_exit(0). Python test asserts all 5 call patterns present. gcc -fsyntax-only exits 0.

title: BR-P2-B2 Build hello.pdb and validate
description: make in sdk/examples/hello_world/ produces hello.pdb >= 104 bytes. validate_pdb(bytes) returns (True,_). Test skips gracefully if riscv32-esp-elf-gcc absent.

title: BR-P2-B3 Load hello.pdb through kernel loader (integration)
description: kernel/tests/hello_world_pdb.rs: std::fs::read hello.pdb, prepare_image into 256KB region, assert Ok(PreparedImage{entry_offset:0}). Gated on integration feature flag; ignored if file absent.

title: AppInfo: add name field from PDB header
description: launcher.rs AppInfo currently only stores the FAT 8.3 filename. Add name: [u8; 32] populated by reading the app_name field (PDB header offset 0x20) so the launcher UI can display human-readable names. Zero-fill when the header cannot be read.

title: scan_apps: surface truncation when >MAX_APPS apps found
description: scan_apps silently caps at MAX_APPS=16 with no signal to the caller. Add a truncated: bool out-parameter or return a ScanResult that includes a truncated flag, so the launcher can show a warning or log when the app directory overflows the list.

title: Phase 5: Emulator + Web Flasher
description: SDL2 desktop emulator with host syscall table; browser-based WebSerial flasher with WASM core

title: EPIC-P5-A: Desktop Emulator
description: SDL2 DisplayTransport backend, host syscall table, .pdb app loader on desktop

title: BR-P5-A1 DisplayBackend trait and SDL2 scaffold
description: Create emulator/src/display.rs with a DisplayBackend trait matching the DisplayTransport interface (write_command, write_data, reset, wait_while_busy). Add SDL2 stub that opens an 800×480 window and presents a blank surface. Test: EmulatorDisplay::new() returns Ok, window title is 'PaperDOS'. Out of scope: pixel rendering, input, .pdb loading.

title: BR-P5-A2 SDL2 display: BW RAM pixel decoder and blit
description: Implement pixel blitting in EmulatorDisplay: on WRITE_RAM_BW data, decode 1-bit rows MSB-first and paint to SDL2 surface (white=1, black=0). Present surface after MASTER_ACTIVATION. Test: write a checkerboard BW payload via the DisplayBackend, capture surface pixels, assert alternating black/white. Out of scope: partial refresh.

title: BR-P5-A3 InputBackend trait and keyboard mapping
description: Create emulator/src/input.rs with InputBackend trait and KeyboardInput impl. Map keys: Right→Right, Left→Left, Up→Up, Down→Down, Enter→Select, Escape→Back, hold threshold→LongPress. Test: inject SDL2 key event, call poll_event(), assert returned ButtonEvent matches expected mapping. Out of scope: .pdb loading.

title: BR-P5-A4 Host FS syscall table wiring
description: Create emulator/src/syscalls/fs.rs implementing all 13 FS syscall slots (fs_open, fs_read, fs_write, fs_close, fs_seek, fs_tell, fs_eof, fs_opendir, fs_readdir, fs_closedir, fs_mkdir, fs_remove, fs_stat) backed by std::fs. Return ENODEV (-6) for unavailable ops. Test: call fs_open on a real temp file, read 4 bytes, assert content matches. Out of scope: display/input syscalls.

title: BR-P5-A5 .pdb loader for emulator (load + relocate)
description: Create emulator/src/loader.rs with load_pdb(path) -> Result<AppImage, _>. Parses 104-byte PDB header (magic, CRC32, sizes), reads reloc table, copies .text+.data to a Vec<u8>, zeros .bss region, applies 32-bit flat relocations. Test with a synthetic minimal .pdb binary; assert entry offset lands in the correct byte. Out of scope: jump-to-app execution.

title: BR-P5-A6 App execution: jump to entry and run loop
description: Add run_app(image, syscalls) in loader.rs: cast entry pointer to extern 'C' fn(*const PdSyscalls), call it, handle return. Wire EmulatorDisplay + KeyboardInput into a HostSyscallTable passed to the app. Test: compile and run the Hello World .pdb from P2-B; assert it returns exit code 0 without panic. Out of scope: OTA, network.

title: BR-P5-A7 Emulator main binary and CLI
description: Create emulator/src/main.rs with argparse: emulator <app.pdb>. Calls load_pdb → build HostSyscallTable → run_app → print exit code. Test: run with Hello World .pdb via cargo run -- hello.pdb; assert exit 0 and SDL2 window shows expected text (visual assertion or pixel probe). Out of scope: debugger attach, step mode.

title: EPIC-P5-B: Web Flasher
description: WASM build of paperdos-core, esptool-js + WebSerial flasher, static hosting + one-click flash UI

title: BR-P5-B1 wasm-pack crate scaffold for paperdos-core
description: Add wasm feature to paperdos-core/Cargo.toml. Add wasm-bindgen dependency gated on feature. Create src/wasm.rs with #[wasm_bindgen] attribute and a hello() -> String function. Test: wasm-pack test --headless --chrome paperdos-core exits 0. Out of scope: actual .tri/.trbk exports.

title: BR-P5-B2 WASM export: decode_tri(bytes: &[u8]) -> Vec<u8>
description: Expose #[wasm_bindgen] pub fn decode_tri(bytes: &[u8]) -> Result<Vec<u8>, JsValue> in wasm.rs. Calls the existing .tri decoder from P4-A3 and returns a flat RGBA pixel buffer (800×480×4 bytes). Test via wasm-bindgen-test: pass a minimal all-white .tri fixture, assert returned Vec length == 800*480*4. Out of scope: trbk, CLI.

title: BR-P5-B3 WASM export: trbk_page_count(bytes: &[u8]) -> u32
description: Expose #[wasm_bindgen] pub fn trbk_page_count(bytes: &[u8]) -> Result<u32, JsValue> calling the P4-A4 header parser. Test via wasm-bindgen-test: pass a minimal .trbk fixture with page_count=3, assert return==3. Out of scope: draw-op decoding.

title: BR-P5-B4 JS firmware manifest fetcher
description: Create web-flasher/src/manifest.js with fetchManifest(url) that GETs a JSON manifest ({version, firmware_url, checksum_sha256}) and returns the parsed object. Test with fetch mock (jest/vitest): assert returned object has expected fields and version is a string. Out of scope: download, WebSerial, UI.

title: BR-P5-B5 JS firmware downloader (arraybuffer fetch)
description: Create web-flasher/src/downloader.js with downloadFirmware(url) -> ArrayBuffer. Uses fetch(url).then(r=>r.arrayBuffer()). Test with fetch mock: assert returned value is an ArrayBuffer with expected byteLength. Out of scope: WebSerial, flash, UI.

title: BR-P5-B6 WebSerial connector module
description: Create web-flasher/src/serial.js with connectSerial() -> SerialPort (calls navigator.serial.requestPort()) and disconnect(port). Mock navigator.serial in tests. Test: connectSerial() calls requestPort once; disconnect() calls port.close(). Include browser-compat warning: display error if navigator.serial is undefined (Firefox). Out of scope: flash, UI.

title: BR-P5-B7 Flash orchestrator (esptool-js wrapper)
description: Create web-flasher/src/flasher.js with flashFirmware(port, firmware: ArrayBuffer, onProgress). Uses esptool-js to erase + write + verify OTA partition. Test: mock ESPLoader, assert flashFirmware calls chip_erase then flash_write with correct args. Out of scope: UI, manifest fetch.

title: BR-P5-B8 Static web flasher UI shell (HTML/JS)
description: Create web-flasher/index.html with: Connect button, Select Firmware dropdown (from manifest), Flash button, progress bar, status text. Wire to manifest.js, downloader.js, serial.js, flasher.js. Test: open in Playwright headless; click Connect (mocked serial); assert Flash button enabled; click Flash; assert progress bar reaches 100%. Out of scope: WASM .tri preview.

title: EPIC-P1-F: Device Integration & First Boot
description: Wire all P1 modules (display, input, storage, syscall table, loader, launcher) into main.rs so the kernel boots, shows the launcher on the SSD1677, and can load and run a .pdb app. This is the integration step that connects all the individually-tested P1 pieces into a runnable device firmware.

title: F1: Display pixel buffer — global DisplayBuf + pixel/clear/rect stubs wired
description: Introduce DisplayBuf([u8; packed 1-bit PANEL_WIDTH*PANEL_HEIGHT]) in kernel/src/display/buf.rs and a critical-section-protected static. Wire pd_display_clear, pd_display_set_pixel, pd_display_draw_rect, pd_display_fill_rect to write into DISPLAY_BUF. Delete old no-op bodies. Test: call pd_display_clear(0x00) assert all buffer bytes 0; call pd_display_set_pixel(0,0,0xFF) assert byte 0 flips.
close_reason: Implemented on main via the retained zero-framebuffer DisplayScene path in kernel/src/syscall/display.rs and covered by the display buffer/scene tests.

title: F2: Display refresh — global transport slot + strip flush from DisplayBuf
description: Add display_set_transport(*mut dyn DisplayTransport) + global slot. Wire pd_display_refresh(mode) to read DISPLAY_BUF, call strip_geometry/pack_strip, and the transport write_data for each strip. Test: register RecordingTransport, call pd_display_refresh(0), assert recording contains WriteRamBW strip sequence.
close_reason: Implemented on main via display_scene_flush_current()/display_refresh_flush() with strip-based SSD1677 flush tests.

title: F3: Launcher app-list renderer render_launcher(buf, apps, state)
description: Add pub fn render_launcher(buf: &mut DisplayBuf, apps: &[AppInfo], state: &LauncherState) to kernel/src/launcher.rs. Draws each app filename as a pixel row; highlights selected row. Pure function — no globals, no transport. Test: 2 apps, selected=1, assert second row pixels differ from first.
close_reason: Implemented on main via launcher::render_launcher(), 8.3 filename formatting, bitmap glyph rendering, and coverage in kernel/tests/launcher_render.rs.

title: F4: Input global slot — poller delegation in input stubs
description: Add input_set_source(*const dyn InputSource) + global slot in kernel/src/syscall/input.rs. Wire pd_input_get_buttons and pd_input_wait_button to delegate through slot (return 0 if None). Delete old no-op bodies. Test: register mock InputSource returning 0b0000_0010, call pd_input_get_buttons(), assert result matches.
close_reason: Implemented via registered input callback slots in kernel/src/syscall/input.rs with coverage in kernel/tests/input_global.rs.

title: F5: Storage global slot — FsState delegation in fs_opendir/fs_readdir stubs
description: Add fs_set_state(*mut FsState) + global slot in kernel/src/syscall/fs.rs. Wire pd_fs_opendir and pd_fs_readdir through the slot. Other FS stubs unchanged for now. Test: register InMemoryBlockDevice-backed FsState, call pd_fs_opendir(""), assert non-null result on known FAT16 image.
close_reason: Implemented via registered filesystem callback slots in kernel/src/syscall/fs.rs with coverage in kernel/tests/fs_global.rs.

title: F6: Launcher loop — run_launcher() -> [u8; 11]
description: Add pub fn run_launcher() -> [u8; 11] in kernel/src/launcher.rs. Calls scan_apps via FS slot, render_launcher into DISPLAY_BUF, pd_display_refresh, pd_input_wait_button, moves LauncherState on Down/Up, returns filename on Select. Test: set up 2-app FAT image + mock input (Down then Select), assert returned filename equals second app.
close_reason: Implemented as launcher::run_launcher(...) with orchestrated selection tests in kernel/tests/launcher_loop.rs.

title: F7: App load + jump — load_and_run(filename, syscalls)
description: Add pub fn load_and_run(filename: &[u8; 11], syscalls: &PdSyscalls) in kernel/src/boot_app.rs. Opens PDB via FS slot, calls pdb::parse_header, loader::load_image, jump::jump_to_app. Test: register FS slot with synthetic valid PDB, call load_and_run, assert jump_to_app entry is reached (via recording or atomic flag).
close_reason: Implemented on main via boot_app::load_and_run() reading a named PDB from FsState and delegating to the low-level loader, with coverage in kernel/tests/boot_app.rs.

title: F8: main.rs — initialize all device globals from esp-hal peripherals
description: In the #[main] block: wire X4DisplayTransport -> display_set_transport, SD SPI -> fs_set_state, ADC poller -> input_set_source. Compile-check test: cargo build --target riscv32imc-unknown-none-elf succeeds with all three globals initialized.
close_reason: Implemented on main by wiring shared SPI2, SSD1677 transport, ADC input poller, SD-backed FsState, and callback slots in kernel/src/main.rs; verified with device-target cargo check.

title: F9: main.rs — replace smoke task with launcher + app run loop
description: Replace runtime_smoke with launcher_task. Loop: filename = run_launcher(); load_and_run(&filename, &build_syscall_table(heap_start, heap_size)). Done when: device flashes, display shows app list, navigation works, hello.pdb runs and exits back to launcher.

title: EPIC-P1-G: App Launch Crash Debugging & Telemetry
description: Urgent hardware debugging epic for the Confirm-triggered app launch crash on X4. Add deterministic diagnostics, instrument the launch path, and resolve the crash so launcher -> app execution is reliable on device.

title: BR-P1-G1 Add device serial logging and panic output
description: Add a real device debug console (USB serial/JTAG) plus panic reporting so launcher/app crashes show deterministic diagnostics instead of freezing silently.

title: BR-P1-G2 Instrument launcher->loader->jump path and add no-jump validation mode
description: Log each launch stage (Confirm, select, open, read, prepare, jump) and add a temporary load-but-dont-jump mode so we can isolate whether the crash happens before or after jump_to_app.

title: BR-P1-G3 Fix Confirm-triggered app launch crash on hardware
description: Use the new diagnostics to reproduce the crash, identify the failing stage, and land a hardware-verified fix so Confirm launches apps without wedging the device.

title: EPIC-P1 Phase 1 foundation
description: Phase 1 moves the Rust kernel from host-only scaffolding into a real ESP32-C3 firmware runtime, beginning with device entry and executor bootstrap before hardware drivers land.
acceptance_criteria:
Done when:
- An ESP32-C3 firmware entry binary exists and cross-compiles.
- Embassy-capable runtime bootstrap is in place for later driver tasks.
- Later Phase 1 driver slices can depend on explicit device/runtime seams.
notes:
Seed tasks:
- BR-P1-01 Add ESP32-C3 device entry smoke
- BR-P1-02 Bootstrap Embassy runtime timer

title: BR-P1-01 Add ESP32-C3 device entry smoke
description:
Why:
- Phase 1 needs a real ESP32-C3 firmware binary before async runtime or hardware driver work can proceed.

In scope:
- Target-gated embedded dependencies in `kernel/Cargo.toml`.
- A `kernel-device` bin target in `kernel/src/main.rs`.
- Minimal `esp-hal` init and device compile smoke.

Out of scope:
- Embassy runtime startup.
- Driver initialization.
- GPIO, SPI, ADC, or SD wiring.
acceptance_criteria:
Done when:
- `cargo check -p kernel --features device --bin kernel-device --target riscv32imc-unknown-none-elf --release` passes.
- `cargo test -p kernel` still passes on host.
notes:
Expected files:
- `kernel/Cargo.toml`
- `kernel/src/main.rs`

title: BR-P1-02 Bootstrap Embassy runtime timer
description:
Why:
- Async driver work depends on a real Embassy-capable runtime bootstrap, not just a blocking device main.

In scope:
- Add `esp-rtos` Embassy runtime wiring for ESP32-C3.
- Initialize the runtime timer source in device startup.
- Keep the main task minimal.

Out of scope:
- Display, input, SD, or filesystem drivers.
- Background tasks beyond runtime smoke.
- UI or launcher work.
acceptance_criteria:
Done when:
- The device binary compiles with runtime bootstrap present.
- Host tests still pass.
- The next hardware driver slice has a concrete async runtime seam.
notes:
Expected files:
- `kernel/Cargo.toml`
- `kernel/src/main.rs`

title: EPIC-P1-B: Button Input (ADC)
description: ADC-based button polling: ButtonEvent enum, threshold decoder for GPIO1/GPIO2, debounce + long-press state machine

title: BR-P1-B1 ButtonId and ButtonEvent type definitions
description: Create kernel/src/input/mod.rs + add pub mod input to lib.rs. Define ButtonId enum (Up, Down, Left, Right, Select, Back) and ButtonEvent enum (Tap(ButtonId), LongPress(ButtonId)), both with Debug/Clone/Copy/PartialEq/Eq. Test: all variants constructable, Tap(Up) != LongPress(Up).

title: BR-P1-B2 AdcSource trait + ScriptedAdc test double
description: kernel/src/input/adc.rs: pub trait AdcSource { type Error; fn read_gpio1 -> Result<u16,E>; fn read_gpio2 -> Result<u16,E>; }. ScriptedAdc holds two &[u16] slices, advances per read, repeats last value when exhausted (Infallible error). Test: scripted sequence returns in-order; past-end repeats last; channels are independent.

title: BR-P1-B3 GPIO1 threshold decoder (decode_gpio1)
description: kernel/src/input/decoder.rs: decode_gpio1(mv: u16) -> Option<ButtonId> using calibrated center-value table from PulpOS/TernReader: Right=3mV±50, Left=1113mV±150, Select=1984mV±150, Back=2556mV±150. Float/no-press -> None. Test: center-of-range for all 4 buttons + float value + boundary tie-break rule (lower window wins).

title: BR-P1-B4 GPIO2 threshold decoder (decode_gpio2)
description: kernel/src/input/decoder.rs: add decode_gpio2(mv: u16) -> Option<ButtonId> using calibrated values from PulpOS: Down=3mV±50, Up=1659mV±150. Float -> None. Test: center-of-range for Down and Up + float. Existing decode_gpio1 tests must still pass.

title: BR-P1-B5 Debounce state machine
description: kernel/src/input/debounce.rs: DebounceFilter { stable, candidate, ticks, threshold_ticks }. update(&mut self, raw: Option<ButtonId>) -> Option<ButtonId>: stable only changes after threshold_ticks consecutive identical readings. Tests: (1) bouncy A/None/A/A/A (threshold=3) emits None until 3 consecutive As; (2) flip A/A/B resets counter; (3) release path: stable returns to None after threshold Nones.

title: BR-P1-B6 Long-press detector
description: kernel/src/input/longpress.rs: LongPressDetector { held, held_ticks, threshold_ticks, fired }. update(stable: Option<ButtonId>) -> Option<ButtonEvent>: emits Tap(b) on first None after short hold; emits LongPress(b) exactly once at threshold_ticks (fired=true suppresses re-fire); fired resets on release. Tests: short hold->Tap; long hold->LongPress no re-fire; no Tap after LongPress; fresh state after release.

title: BR-P1-B7 InputPoller compose all layers
description: kernel/src/input/poller.rs: InputPoller<A: AdcSource> { adc, db1: DebounceFilter, db2: DebounceFilter, lp: LongPressDetector }. poll() -> Result<Option<ButtonEvent>, A::Error>: reads both channels, decodes, debounces each independently (GPIO1 priority when both active), feeds LongPressDetector. Tests with ScriptedAdc: stable GPIO1 Up->Tap(Up); stable GPIO2 Down->Tap(Down); long hold->LongPress; both-active->GPIO1 wins. No Embassy wiring yet.

title: EPIC-P1-C: SD Card + FAT Filesystem
description: SD init via embedded-sdmmc, SPI bus arbitration, FAT file ops (open/read/write/seek/dir) needed by syscall table

title: BR-P1-C1 StorageError type + MockSpi test double
description: Create kernel/src/storage/mod.rs + error.rs with StorageError enum (NotReady, IoError, NotFound, NoSpace, etc.). MockSpi holds reply bytes + sent-log implementing embedded-hal SpiDevice. Test: transfer sends and receives correct bytes.

title: BR-P1-C10 CriticalSectionDevice wiring for shared SPI
description: FsState::from_spi2(CriticalSectionDevice<Spi2>) constructor gated on #[cfg(target_arch=riscv32)]. host tests still pass. cargo check-kernel-embedded exits 0.

title: BR-P1-C1 StorageError type + MockSpi test double
description: Create kernel/src/storage/mod.rs + error.rs with StorageError enum (NotReady, IoError, NotFound, NoSpace, etc.). MockSpi holds reply bytes + sent-log implementing embedded-hal SpiDevice. Test: transfer sends and receives correct bytes.

title: BR-P1-C2 SD card SPI init sequence (CMD0/CMD8/ACMD41)
description: SdCard<SPI> struct + init() -> Result<CardKind>. Sends GO_IDLE, SEND_IF_COND, ACMD41/CMD58. Test: MockSpi pre-loaded with SDHC negotiation -> Ok(Sdhc); busy-loop sequence -> Err(NotReady).

title: BR-P1-C3 SD card block read/write (CMD17/CMD24)
description: SdCard: read_block(lba, buf) and write_block(lba, buf). Data token 0xFE handling. Test: MockSpi pre-loaded with CMD17 exchange -> buf filled; write_block records 512 payload bytes.

title: BR-P1-C4 BlockDevice adapter over SdCard
description: SdBlockDevice<SPI> implementing embedded-sdmmc::BlockDevice. read/write/num_blocks. Test: read at block 0 returns expected data; write triggers correct SPI exchange.

title: BR-P1-C5 VolumeManager construction and volume open
description: FsState<SPI> with VolumeManager from SdBlockDevice + NoopTimeSource. FsState::new opens partition 0. Test: MockBlockDevice with canned MBR+FAT32 boot sector -> Ok.

title: BR-P1-C6 fs_open and fs_close
description: FileHandle backed by 8-slot open-file table. fs_open(path, write) -> Result<FileHandle>; fs_close. Test: open existing -> handle 0; close frees slot; open nonexistent -> Err(NotFound).

title: BR-P1-C7 fs_read, fs_write, fs_seek, fs_tell, fs_eof
description: FileHandle I/O ops via embedded-sdmmc File. PD_SEEK_SET/CUR/END. Test: read correct bytes; seek/tell round-trip; write+read round-trip; eof correct at end.

title: BR-P1-C8 fs_mkdir, fs_remove, fs_stat
description: PdStat {entry_type, size, mtime}. Test: mkdir succeeds; stat returns correct size/type; remove then stat -> Err(NotFound).

title: BR-P1-C9 fs_opendir, fs_readdir, fs_closedir
description: DirHandle backed by 4-slot open-dir table. readdir -> bool. Test: 2-entry root dir: readdir x2 returns entries; x3 returns false; closedir frees slot.

title: BR-P1-C2 SD card SPI init sequence (CMD0/CMD8/ACMD41)
description: SdCard<SPI> struct + init() -> Result<CardKind>. Sends GO_IDLE, SEND_IF_COND, ACMD41/CMD58. Test: MockSpi pre-loaded with SDHC negotiation -> Ok(Sdhc); busy-loop sequence -> Err(NotReady).

title: BR-P1-C10 CriticalSectionDevice shared-SPI wiring
description: FsState::from_spi2(CriticalSectionDevice<Spi2>) gated on cfg(riscv32). Host tests unaffected. cargo check-kernel-embedded exits 0.

title: BR-P1-C3 SD card block read/write (CMD17/CMD24)
description: SdCard: read_block(lba, buf) and write_block(lba, buf). Data token 0xFE handling. Test: MockSpi pre-loaded with CMD17 exchange -> buf filled correctly; write_block records 512 payload bytes.

title: BR-P1-C4 BlockDevice adapter over SdCard
description: SdBlockDevice<SPI> implementing embedded-sdmmc::BlockDevice trait. read/write/num_blocks delegating to SdCard. Test: BlockDevice::read at block 0 returns expected data; write triggers correct SPI exchange.

title: BR-P1-C5 VolumeManager construction and volume open
description: FsState<SPI> holding VolumeManager from SdBlockDevice + NoopTimeSource. FsState::new opens partition 0. Test: MockBlockDevice with canned MBR+FAT32 boot sector -> FsState::new returns Ok.

title: BR-P1-C6 fs_open and fs_close
description: FileHandle backed by fixed-size open-file table (8 slots). fs_open(path, write) -> Result<FileHandle>; fs_close(handle). Test: open existing file -> handle 0; close frees slot; open nonexistent -> Err(NotFound).

title: BR-P1-C7 fs_read, fs_write, fs_seek, fs_tell, fs_eof
description: FileHandle I/O operations delegating to embedded-sdmmc File. PD_SEEK_SET/CUR/END constants. Test: read returns known bytes; seek-to-0 then tell=0; write then read-back round-trip; eof true only after last byte.

title: BR-P1-C8 fs_mkdir, fs_remove, fs_stat
description: Mutation and metadata ops. PdStat {entry_type, size, mtime}. Test: mkdir succeeds; stat returns correct size/type; remove then stat -> Err(NotFound).

title: BR-P1-C9 fs_opendir, fs_readdir, fs_closedir
description: DirHandle backed by fixed-size open-dir table (4 slots). readdir -> bool (false=exhausted). Test: root dir with 2 entries: readdir x2 returns entries; readdir x3 returns false; closedir frees slot.

title: EPIC-P1-D: Syscall Table
description: PdSyscalls #[repr(C)] struct with ~70 extern C function pointers; wires display, input, FS, memory, font, system syscalls

title: BR-P1-D1 PdSyscalls struct skeleton with stub slots
description: kernel/src/syscall/mod.rs: PdSyscalls #[repr(C)] with all ~70 fields as stub_unimplemented fn pointers (return -1). abi_version=1. SyscallTable::build(heap_start, heap_size). Test: abi_version==1; size_of::<PdSyscalls>()==16+52*4; app_heap fields round-trip.

title: BR-P1-D10 Full SyscallTable integration test + fixed-address export
description: kernel/tests/syscall_table.rs: build full table, assert abi_version==1, size correct, all fn-pointers non-null, fs slots differ from net stubs. Add #[link_section=.syscall_table] static export gated on cfg(riscv32). cargo check-kernel-embedded exits 0.

title: BR-P1-D2 sys group syscalls
description: kernel/src/syscall/sys.rs: pd_sys_sleep_ms, pd_sys_millis (AtomicU32 tick injectable), pd_sys_exit (spin), pd_sys_reboot (spin), pd_sys_log (no-op), pd_sys_get_free_heap, pd_sys_wifi_acquire (returns -1), pd_sys_wifi_release. Test: millis returns injected tick; log does not panic.

title: BR-P1-D3 display-basic syscalls (clear, refresh, rotation, dimensions)

title: EPIC-P0 Phase 0 foundation
description: Phase 0 creates the smallest safe foundation for PaperDOS execution work: a Rust workspace, locked ABI and .pdb contracts, a host-side loader dry run, explicit platform seams, and CI guardrails.
design: Phase 0 is intentionally library-first and host-testable. It excludes hardware drivers, firmware flashing, and web flasher work so later phases can land on a stable contract and verification base.
acceptance_criteria:
Done when:
- The `kernel` crate cross-checks for `riscv32imc-unknown-none-elf`.
- ABI v1 layout is locked against `sdk/include/paperdos.h`.
- `.pdb` parsing and validation work on host.
- A dry-run loader prepares an image without execution.
- `br ready -l phase-0` exposes only the next intended task.
notes:
Child epics:
- EPIC-P0-A Workspace Bootstrap
- EPIC-P0-B ABI Freeze
- EPIC-P0-C PDB Parser
- EPIC-P0-D Loader Dry Run
- EPIC-P0-E Platform Seams
- EPIC-P0-F Guardrails

title: EPIC-P0-A Workspace Bootstrap
description: Create the minimal Rust workspace and target configuration needed for Phase 0 host and cross-target checks.
design: Each child task is a single strict red-green slice. Parent-child links group the work; task-to-task `blocks` links enforce execution order.
acceptance_criteria:
Done when:
- Root and `kernel` Cargo manifests exist.
- `kernel` compiles as a minimal `#![no_std]` library.
- The repo can run `cargo check -p kernel --lib --target riscv32imc-unknown-none-elf`.
notes:
Child tasks:
- BR-P0-01
- BR-P0-02
- BR-P0-03
- BR-P0-04

title: BR-P0-01 Create standalone kernel crate manifest
description:
Why:
- The repo needs a Rust package before any host or cross-target loop can exist.

In scope:
- A standalone `kernel` crate manifest.
- Package and library metadata only.

Out of scope:
- Root workspace wiring.
- Toolchain config.
- Source files.
design:
Strict TDD:
1. Add or update one targeted failing test first: a standalone metadata smoke check for `kernel/Cargo.toml`.
2. Run `cargo metadata --manifest-path kernel/Cargo.toml --no-deps` and confirm it fails because the manifest is absent.
3. Implement the minimum production change needed to make it pass.
4. Re-run `cargo metadata --manifest-path kernel/Cargo.toml --no-deps`.
5. Run `cargo metadata --manifest-path kernel/Cargo.toml --format-version 1`.
acceptance_criteria:
Done when:
- `cargo metadata --manifest-path kernel/Cargo.toml --no-deps` passes.
- Cargo recognizes `kernel` as a standalone package.
notes:
Expected files:
- `kernel/Cargo.toml`

Depends on:
- none

Unlocks:
- BR-P0-02

- Syscall implementations.

5. Run `cargo test -p kernel abi_layout`.

title: EPIC-P1-A Display Bring-up
description: Phase 1 display bring-up covers the smallest executable path from SSD1677 controller contracts to DMA-backed device init, while keeping rendering, partial refresh, and board wiring split into strict micro-slices.
design: Each child task is a single red-green loop with one primary seam. Parent-child links group the display work; task-to-task `blocks` links enforce the execution order so only the next intended slice is ready.
acceptance_criteria:
Done when:
- SSD1677 constants, transport, strip rendering, partial refresh, and device wiring all have explicit narrow tasks.
- The dependency chain exposes only the first display bring-up task as ready.
- The final task compiles the DMA-backed device init smoke path.
notes:
Child epics:
- EPIC-P1-A1 Display Contracts
- EPIC-P1-A2 Strip Rendering
- EPIC-P1-A3 Partial Refresh
- EPIC-P1-A4 Device Wiring

Out of scope:
- Transport behavior.
- Init sequencing.
- Device pin wiring.
design:
Strict TDD:
1. Add or update one targeted failing test first.
2. Run `cargo test -p kernel ssd1677_constants` and confirm it fails for missing constants.
3. Implement the minimum production change needed to make it pass.
4. Re-run `cargo test -p kernel ssd1677_constants`.
5. Run `cargo test -p kernel display_transport`.
acceptance_criteria:

- `cargo test -p kernel ssd1677_constants` passes.

title: BR-P1-17 Add partial-refresh promotion counter
description:
Why:
- Ghosting-control policy should be isolated from low-level command transport.

In scope:
- Counter or state helper that promotes partial refreshes to a required full refresh after a threshold.
- Threshold and reset behavior only.

Out of scope:
- Actual refresh command emission.
- Image diffing.
- Launcher policy.
design:
Strict TDD:
1. Add or update one targeted failing test first.
2. Run `cargo test -p kernel partial_refresh_promotion` and confirm it fails because the counter helper is missing.
3. Implement the minimum production change needed to make it pass.
4. Re-run `cargo test -p kernel partial_refresh_promotion`.
5. Run `cargo test -p kernel display_refresh`.
acceptance_criteria:
Done when:
- `cargo test -p kernel partial_refresh_promotion` passes.
- The driver has one deterministic promotion policy seam.
notes:

- `kernel/src/display/refresh.rs`
- `kernel/tests/display_refresh.rs`

title: Phase 3: Store + Networking
description: WiFi integration, HTTP client, Wi-Fi upload server, Store app with manifest + download, OTA kernel update

title: EPIC-P3-A: WiFi Integration
description: WiFi config from SD config.json, net_wifi_connect/disconnect/status syscalls, sys_wifi_release/acquire memory reclaim

title: BR-P3-A1 WiFiDriver trait and MockWifiDriver
description: kernel/src/net/wifi_driver.rs: WiFiDriver trait with connect/disconnect/status; WifiStatus enum (Disconnected/Connecting/Connected); WifiError. MockWifiDriver behind cfg(test). Test: connect transitions status to Connecting.

title: BR-P3-A2 WifiManager state machine
description: kernel/src/net/wifi_manager.rs: WifiManager<D: WiFiDriver> drives Idle->Connecting->Connected->Idle. Test: connect-while-connected returns error; disconnect-from-Idle is no-op; status reflects state.

title: BR-P3-A3 WiFi config reader from JSON
description: kernel/src/config/mod.rs: ConfigReader trait + MockConfigReader. Parse JSON bytes with serde-json-core -> WifiCreds{ssid: String<32>, pass: String<64>}. Test: valid JSON parses; missing ssid -> MissingField; malformed -> ParseError.

title: BR-P3-A4 net_wifi_connect syscall wired to WifiManager
description: kernel/src/syscall/net.rs: net_wifi_connect(ssid_ptr,ssid_len,pass_ptr,pass_len)->i32 via MockWifiDriver. Test: valid call->0; connect-while-connected->-EALREADY; null ptr->-EINVAL.

title: BR-P3-A5 net_wifi_disconnect and net_wifi_status syscalls
description: Extend net.rs: net_wifi_disconnect()->i32; net_wifi_status()->i32 (0/1/2). Test: status returns correct int per enum variant; disconnect transitions state; disconnect-from-idle returns 0.

title: BR-P3-A6 sys_wifi_acquire and sys_wifi_release
description: WifiMemoryGate trait + mock. WifiManager::acquire/release gates ~64KB. Test: acquire-while-acquired->-EBUSY; release-when-not-acquired->-EINVAL; acquire+release round-trip->0.

title: EPIC-P3-B: HTTP Client + Dev Push Server
description: net_http_get, streaming net_http_begin/send/read/end, Wi-Fi HTTP upload server with mDNS

title: BR-P3-B1 HttpClient trait and MockHttpClient
description: kernel/src/net/http_client.rs: HttpClient trait with get(url,buf)->Result<usize> and streaming begin/set_header/send/read/status_code/end. MockHttpClient with canned responses. Test: get returns correct byte count and buffer.

title: BR-P3-B2 net_http_get syscall
description: net_http_get(url_ptr,url_len,buf_ptr,buf_size)->i32 via MockHttpClient. Test: fits in buffer->byte count; oversized->-ENOBUFS; null url->-EINVAL.

title: BR-P3-B3 Streaming HTTP syscalls (begin through end)
description: net_http_begin/set_header/send/read/status_code/end with 4-slot handle table. Test: full round-trip returns correct bytes/status; 5th handle->-EMFILE; read after end->-EBADF.

title: BR-P3-B4 Dev push server request parser
description: PushRequestParser::parse(buf) -> Result<PushRequest{filename,body_offset}>. Parse raw HTTP POST /upload, Content-Disposition filename. Test: valid -> correct filename+offset; wrong path -> WrongPath; missing filename -> MissingFilename.

title: BR-P3-B5 Dev push server file writer seam
description: PushFileWriter trait + MockPushFileWriter. PushServer::handle_request(buf, writer). Test: valid request calls write_app with correct filename+body; oversized filename -> FilenameTooLong; writer error propagates.

title: BR-P3-B6 mDNS advertisement stub and wiring
description: MdnsAdvertiser trait + mock. PushServer::start calls advertise(paperdos, 80). Test: start calls advertise with correct args; stop calls stop; failing advertiser propagates error.

title: EPIC-P3-C: Store App
description: Manifest JSON fetch + parse, scrollable store list UI, app download + install with CRC32 verify

title: BR-P3-C1 AppManifest struct and JSON deserialisation
description: kernel/src/store/manifest.rs: AppManifest with heapless::String fields (id,name,version,abi_version,description,size_bytes,download_url,checksum,category). serde::Deserialize via serde-json-core. Test: valid JSON -> correct struct; missing checksum -> error; extra fields ignored.

title: BR-P3-C2 Manifest list fetcher and parser
description: ManifestFetcher trait + MockManifestFetcher. ManifestListParser::parse(buf) -> Vec<AppManifest,16>. Test: 3-item array -> len=3 correct ids; >16 items -> TooManyApps; empty -> empty vec.

title: BR-P3-C3 Store list UI model
description: StoreListModel{manifests, cursor} with scroll_up/scroll_down (clamped), selected_app(), render(framebuf). Test: scroll past ends does not overflow; selected_app correct after moves; render writes non-zero to mock framebuffer.

title: BR-P3-C4 CRC32 verifier
description: verify_crc32(data, expected_hex) -> Result<(),CrcError> using no_std CRC32. Test: correct checksum -> Ok; flipped bit -> Mismatch; malformed hex -> InvalidHex.

title: BR-P3-C5 App downloader with CRC verification
description: AppDownloader{HttpClient, FileWriter trait}. download(manifest, writer): stream URL in 512B chunks, verify_crc32 on complete blob; on mismatch call writer.delete_app and return CrcMismatch. Test: happy path->Ok; mismatch->deletes file+error; HTTP error->propagates.

title: BR-P3-C6 App index registration (appindex.json)
description: AppIndex{load(reader), register(manifest), save(writer)} with IndexReader/IndexWriter traits. JSON serialised via serde-json-core. Test: load parses existing index; register appends; duplicate id updates; save round-trips.

title: BR-P3-C7 Store app end-to-end wiring (select and download)
description: StoreApp::run(input, http, writer, index) -> Result<(),StoreError>. Orchestrates fetch->render->navigate->download->register. Test: Down+Select downloads second app and registers; Back returns UserExit; download failure leaves index unchanged.

title: EPIC-P3-D: OTA Kernel Update
description: OTA manifest check, firmware download to inactive partition, rollback on boot failure

title: BR-P3-D1 OtaManifest struct and JSON deserialisation
description: kernel/src/ota/manifest.rs: OtaManifest{version:String<16>, download_url:String<128>, size:u32, sha256:String<64>}. Test: valid JSON -> correct; missing sha256 -> error; size as string -> error.

title: BR-P3-D2 Version comparator (semver MAJOR.MINOR.PATCH)
description: is_newer(current, candidate) -> Result<bool, VersionError>. Test: 1.2.0 vs 1.3.0 -> true; same -> false; 2.0.0 vs 1.9.9 -> false; malformed -> InvalidFormat.

title: BR-P3-D3 OTA manifest fetcher and version check
description: OtaChecker{HttpClient, current_version}.check() -> Result<OtaCheckResult>. OtaCheckResult: UpToDate | UpdateAvailable(OtaManifest). Test: newer -> UpdateAvailable; same -> UpToDate; HTTP error -> FetchError; bad JSON -> ParseError.

title: BR-P3-D4 SHA256 verifier for firmware blob
description: verify_sha256(data, expected_hex) -> Result<(),Sha256Error> via no_std sha2 crate. Test: correct hash -> Ok; one-byte mutation -> Mismatch; 63-char hex -> InvalidHex.

title: BR-P3-D5 OtaPartitionWriter trait and mock
description: OtaPartitionWriter trait: begin/write_chunk/commit/abort. MockPartitionWriter records chunks. Test: chunks between begin+commit recorded; write before begin -> NotStarted; write after commit -> AlreadyCommitted.

title: BR-P3-D6 OTA downloader with SHA256 and partition write
description: OtaDownloader{HttpClient, OtaPartitionWriter}.download(manifest, partition). Streams 512B chunks, accumulates for SHA256, calls verify_sha256; mismatch -> abort + ChecksumMismatch. Test: happy path calls commit; SHA256 mismatch calls abort; HTTP mid-stream error calls abort.

title: BR-P3-D7 Boot-count watchdog state machine
description: BootWatchdog{WatchdogStore}.increment_and_check() -> BootAction (Continue|Revert if count>=3); mark_healthy() clears count. Test: 0->1 returns Continue; count at 3 -> Revert; mark_healthy clears; store read error propagates.

title: BR-P3-D8 OTA orchestrator (check, download, reboot)
description: OtaOrchestrator::run(http, partition, reboot: RebootTrigger) -> Result<OtaOutcome>. UpToDate -> AlreadyCurrent; UpdateAvailable -> download -> commit -> reboot. Test: up-to-date skips partition; update calls commit+reboot; download failure returns error without reboot.
